crpc_title: Definitions
bnss_title: Definitions
description: Defines key terms used throughout the criminal procedure code such as bailable/non-bailable offence, cognizable offence, complaint, investigation and inquiry.
key_changes: Definitions retained and modernised. New terms like 'audio-video electronic means' and 'electronic communication' added to support digital procedure.

crpc_title: Classes of Criminal Courts
bnss_title: Classes of Criminal Courts
description: Sets out the hierarchy of criminal courts: Courts of Session, Judicial Magistrates and Executive Magistrates.
key_changes: Structure retained. Metropolitan Magistrate courts abolished; a single class of Judicial Magistrate now applies across the country.

offence: Power of Superior Officers of Police
crpc_title: Powers of superior officers of police
bnss_title: Powers of superior officers of police
description: Superior police officers may exercise the same powers as an officer in charge of a police station throughout the local area to which they are appointed.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: When police may arrest without warrant
bnss_title: When police may arrest without warrant
description: Lists the situations in which a police officer may arrest a person without an order from a magistrate and without a warrant, including cognizable offences.
key_changes: Safeguards strengthened. For offences punishable up to 7 years, no arrest of an infirm person or a person above 60 years without prior permission of an officer not below DSP rank.

crpc_title: Notice of appearance before police officer
bnss_title: Notice of appearance before police officer
description: Where arrest is not required, the police must issue a notice directing the person to appear; the person must comply and cannot be arrested if they do so, unless recorded reasons exist.
key_changes: Retained with added safeguards for arrest of elderly and infirm persons.

crpc_title: Arrest how made
bnss_title: Arrest how made
description: Explains the manner of making an arrest — actual touch or confinement of the body unless the person submits to custody.
key_changes: NEW: Use of handcuffs now expressly permitted for habitual/repeat offenders and specified serious offences (BNSS 43(3)). Women can generally not be arrested after sunset and before sunrise.

offence: Right to Know Grounds of Arrest
crpc_title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and of right to bail
bnss_title: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and of right to bail
description: Every arrested person must be told the full particulars of the offence and, in a bailable case, of their right to be released on bail.
key_changes: Retained. Reinforced by the duty to inform family/friends of the arrest.

offence: Right to Meet Advocate During Interrogation
crpc_title: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate
bnss_title: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation
description: An arrested person is entitled to meet an advocate of their choice during interrogation, though not throughout.
key_changes: Right retained and placed with the arrest provisions.

crpc_title: Examination of arrested person by medical officer
bnss_title: Examination of arrested person by medical officer
description: An arrested person shall be examined by a medical officer soon after arrest to record any injuries and their condition.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Person arrested not to be detained more than twenty-four hours
bnss_title: Person arrested not to be detained more than twenty-four hours
description: No police officer shall detain an arrested person for more than 24 hours (excluding travel time) without producing them before a magistrate.
key_changes: Constitutional safeguard retained unchanged.

crpc_title: Summons to produce document or other thing
bnss_title: Summons to produce document or other thing
description: A court or officer in charge of a police station may issue a summons or written order to produce any document or thing necessary for an investigation, inquiry or trial.
key_changes: Extended to expressly cover documents and devices held in electronic form.

crpc_title: When search-warrant may be issued
bnss_title: When search-warrant may be issued
description: A court may issue a search warrant where a person will not produce a document, where the thing is not known to be in any particular person's possession, or where a general search is needed.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Persons in charge of closed place to allow search
bnss_title: Persons in charge of closed place to allow search
description: The occupant of a place being searched must allow free entry and the search must be conducted before independent witnesses.
key_changes: Retained; complemented by mandatory audio-video recording of searches under BNSS 105.

crpc_title: Power of police officer to seize certain property
bnss_title: Power of police officer to seize certain property
description: A police officer may seize any property alleged or suspected to have been stolen or found in circumstances creating suspicion of an offence.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: No equivalent in CrPC
bnss_title: Recording of search and seizure through audio-video electronic means
description: Search and seizure, and the preparation of the list of seized items, must be recorded through audio-video electronic means, preferably a mobile phone, and forwarded to the magistrate.
key_changes: BRAND NEW provision. There is no CrPC equivalent — introduces mandatory videography to prevent tampering and false planting of evidence.

crpc_title: Security for keeping the peace in other cases
bnss_title: Security for keeping the peace in other cases
description: An Executive Magistrate may require a person likely to breach the peace to show cause why they should not execute a bond for keeping the peace.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
bnss_title: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
description: A person with sufficient means who neglects or refuses to maintain their wife, children or parents unable to maintain themselves may be ordered by a magistrate to pay a monthly allowance.
key_changes: Substantively retained. Renumbered from 125 to 144.

crpc_title: Conditional order for removal of nuisance
bnss_title: Conditional order for removal of nuisance
description: A magistrate may make a conditional order requiring the removal of an unlawful obstruction or nuisance from a public place.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

offence: Urgent Orders in Cases of Nuisance / Danger
crpc_title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger
bnss_title: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger
description: A District/Executive Magistrate may issue orders to prevent obstruction, annoyance, injury, or a disturbance of public tranquillity — commonly used to prohibit assembly of persons.
key_changes: The famous 'Section 144' is now Section 163 of BNSS. Powers substantively unchanged.

crpc_title: Arrest to prevent the commission of cognizable offences
bnss_title: Arrest to prevent the commission of cognizable offences
description: A police officer may arrest, without orders from a magistrate, a person designing to commit a cognizable offence if it cannot otherwise be prevented.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Information in cognizable cases
bnss_title: Information in cognizable cases
description: Information relating to the commission of a cognizable offence must be recorded by the police as a First Information Report (FIR).
key_changes: MAJOR CHANGE: FIR can now be registered electronically (e-FIR) and irrespective of jurisdiction (Zero FIR). For offences punishable between 3 and 7 years, a preliminary enquiry within 14 days may precede registration.

crpc_title: Information as to non-cognizable cases and investigation of such cases
bnss_title: Information as to non-cognizable cases and investigation of such cases
description: In non-cognizable cases the police record the information and refer the informant to a magistrate; investigation needs the magistrate's order.
key_changes: Retained with a duty to record and forward such information.

crpc_title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
bnss_title: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
description: An officer in charge of a police station may investigate a cognizable case without the order of a magistrate; a magistrate may also order such investigation.
key_changes: Retained. A magistrate may now require a preliminary consideration and the police officer's supervisory response before ordering investigation.

crpc_title: Procedure for investigation
bnss_title: Procedure for investigation
description: Sets out the steps the police take on receiving information of a cognizable offence — proceeding to the spot, investigating facts and taking measures for arrest.
key_changes: MAJOR CHANGE: Forensic investigation and videography of the crime scene are now mandatory for offences punishable with 7 years or more.

crpc_title: Examination of witnesses by police
bnss_title: Examination of witnesses by police
description: The police may examine orally any person supposed to be acquainted with the facts of the case and record their statements.
key_changes: NEW: Statements may be recorded by audio-video electronic means. Statements of women against whom certain offences are alleged shall be recorded by a woman officer.

crpc_title: Recording of confessions and statements
bnss_title: Recording of confessions and statements
description: A magistrate may record confessions and statements made during investigation, with safeguards to ensure they are voluntary.
key_changes: Retained with expanded use of audio-video recording for statements of vulnerable victims and witnesses.

crpc_title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
bnss_title: Procedure when investigation cannot be completed in twenty-four hours
description: Where investigation cannot be finished in 24 hours, the accused is produced before a magistrate who may authorise detention (police or judicial custody).
key_changes: MAJOR CHANGE: The 15-day police custody may now be sought in parts spread across the first 40 or 60 days of the total investigation period, rather than only the first 15 days.

crpc_title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation
bnss_title: Report of police officer on completion of investigation
description: On completing the investigation the police file a final report (chargesheet) before the magistrate empowered to take cognizance.
key_changes: MAJOR CHANGE: Investigation to be completed and chargesheet filed within 90 days (extendable by 90). The informant must be updated on progress within 90 days, including by electronic means.

crpc_title: Inquiry by Magistrate into cause of death
bnss_title: Inquiry by Magistrate into cause of death
description: A magistrate holds an inquiry into deaths in police custody, or of persons in custody, and other specified deaths.
key_changes: Retained. Mandatory magisterial inquiry for deaths and rape in police custody reinforced.

crpc_title: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates
bnss_title: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates
description: A magistrate may take cognizance of an offence on a complaint, on a police report, or on information/own knowledge.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Prosecution of Judges and public servants
bnss_title: Prosecution of Judges and public servants
description: Prior sanction of the government is required to prosecute judges, magistrates and public servants for acts done in discharge of official duty.
key_changes: NEW: A decision on the grant of sanction must be taken within 120 days of the request, failing which sanction is deemed granted.

crpc_title: Examination of complainant
bnss_title: Examination of complainant
description: A magistrate taking cognizance on a complaint examines the complainant and witnesses on oath.
key_changes: NEW: A public servant cannot be proceeded against on a complaint without giving them an opportunity to be heard, and their superior's report.

crpc_title: Postponement of issue of process
bnss_title: Postponement of issue of process
description: A magistrate may postpone issuing process against the accused and inquire into the case, or direct an investigation, to decide whether there is sufficient ground to proceed.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Supply to the accused of copy of police report and other documents
bnss_title: Supply to the accused of copy of police report and other documents
description: The accused must be given free copies of the police report, FIR, statements and other documents the prosecution relies on.
key_changes: NEW: Copies must be supplied within 14 days and may be furnished in electronic form.

crpc_title: Commitment of case to Court of Session when offence is triable exclusively by it
bnss_title: Commitment of case to Court of Session
description: Where an offence is triable exclusively by the Court of Session, the magistrate commits the case to that court.
key_changes: NEW: Commitment to be made within 90 days of taking cognizance (extendable for reasons recorded).

crpc_title: Discharge
bnss_title: Discharge
description: If, on considering the record, the judge finds no sufficient ground to proceed, the accused is discharged.
key_changes: NEW: An application for discharge must be filed within 60 days of committal.

crpc_title: Framing of charge
bnss_title: Framing of charge
description: If there is ground to presume the accused committed the offence, the judge frames a charge in writing.
key_changes: Retained with fixed timelines to speed up trials.

crpc_title: Judgment of acquittal or conviction
bnss_title: Judgment of acquittal or conviction
description: After hearing arguments the court delivers a judgment either acquitting or convicting the accused.
key_changes: NEW: Judgment must be pronounced within 30 days (extendable to 45) of completion of arguments.

crpc_title: Power to postpone or adjourn proceedings
bnss_title: Power to postpone or adjourn proceedings
description: The court may postpone or adjourn an inquiry or trial, recording reasons, but must ordinarily proceed day-to-day once examination of witnesses begins.
key_changes: NEW: No more than two adjournments may be granted to a party, and only after hearing the opposite side.

crpc_title: Power to examine the accused
bnss_title: Power to examine the accused
description: The court questions the accused on the circumstances appearing in evidence against them, to enable a personal explanation.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Compounding of offences
bnss_title: Compounding of offences
description: Lists offences that may be settled (compounded) between the parties, some with the court's permission, leading to acquittal.
key_changes: Updated to refer to the new BNS section numbers for each compoundable offence.

crpc_title: No equivalent in CrPC
bnss_title: Inquiry, trial or judgment in absentia of proclaimed offender
description: A proclaimed offender who has absconded to evade trial may be tried, and judgment pronounced, in their absence after specified safeguards and notice.
key_changes: BRAND NEW provision. The CrPC had no full trial-in-absentia mechanism — this allows completion of trial against fugitives such as absconding economic offenders.

crpc_title: Order to pay compensation
bnss_title: Order to pay compensation
description: The court imposing a fine may order that it be applied towards compensating a victim for loss or injury caused by the offence.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Victim compensation scheme
bnss_title: Victim compensation scheme
description: State governments must prepare a scheme to provide funds for compensating victims who need rehabilitation, in coordination with the Central scheme.
key_changes: Retained and reinforced, with witness protection scheme obligations elsewhere in BNSS.

offence: Release on Probation of Good Conduct
crpc_title: Order to release on probation of good conduct or after admonition
bnss_title: Order to release on probation of good conduct or after admonition
description: The court may release certain first-time or minor offenders on probation of good conduct instead of sentencing them at once.
key_changes: Retained. BNSS also introduces community service as a distinct form of punishment for petty offences.

crpc_title: Appeals from convictions
bnss_title: Appeals from convictions
description: Provides the right of appeal to a convicted person, to the High Court or Court of Session depending on the sentencing court.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Suspension of sentence pending the appeal; release of appellant on bail
bnss_title: Suspension of sentence pending the appeal; release of appellant on bail
description: The appellate court may suspend the execution of the sentence and release the convicted appellant on bail while the appeal is pending.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: In what cases bail to be taken
bnss_title: In what cases bail to be taken
description: In a bailable offence the accused has a right to be released on bail by the police or the court.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Maximum period for which an undertrial prisoner can be detained
bnss_title: Maximum period for which an undertrial prisoner can be detained
description: An undertrial who has been detained for half the maximum sentence for the offence must be released on bond.
key_changes: NEW: A first-time offender (never convicted before) is entitled to release on bond after serving one-third of the maximum period of detention.

crpc_title: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence
bnss_title: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence
description: Sets out when a court may grant bail in a non-bailable offence and the conditions and restrictions that may apply.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest
bnss_title: Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest
description: A person who apprehends arrest for a non-bailable offence may seek anticipatory bail from the High Court or Court of Session.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered from 438 to 482.

offence: Special Powers of High Court / Sessions Court re Bail
crpc_title: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail
bnss_title: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail
description: The High Court or Court of Session has wide powers to grant bail, impose conditions, and set aside or modify bail conditions.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: Bar to taking cognizance after lapse of the period of limitation
bnss_title: Bar to taking cognizance after lapse of the period of limitation
description: Bars a court from taking cognizance of certain minor offences after the limitation period, which depends on the maximum punishment.
key_changes: No substantive change. Renumbered.

crpc_title: No equivalent in CrPC
bnss_title: Trials and proceedings to be held in electronic mode
description: All trials, inquiries and proceedings — including issue of summons, examination of witnesses and recording of evidence — may be held in electronic mode.
key_changes: BRAND NEW provision enabling end-to-end digital criminal proceedings, with no direct CrPC counterpart.
